name: Gabrielle's Crawfish Enchiladas
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Green bell pepper, finely chopped
1/4 cup Fish stock or clam juice
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the sauce by melting lard in a medium saucepan.
2. Add chili powder, onion, bell pepper, celery, and garlic to the pan.
3. Cook over medium heat until the vegetables are soft, about 10 minutes.
4. Stir in corn flour (or Wondra) and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
5. Add cream and fish stock; bring the mixture to a boil.
6. Stir in cheddar cheese until it is fully melted and bubbly.
7. Remove the sauce from heat and season with salt as needed.
8. Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
9. Soften the tortilla shells by wrapping them in plastic wrap and heating in a microwave for 30 to 50 seconds.
10. Mix half of the sauce with the cooked crawfish tails.
11. Fill each tortilla shell with about 1/4 cup of the crawfish mixture, adding green onion on top.
12. Roll up the shells tightly and place them snugly into a small oven casserole dish.
13. Pour the remaining sauce over the enchiladas and sprinkle shredded Monterey Jack cheese on top.
14. Bake until the enchiladas are heated through and the cheese is melted and bubbly, about 10 minutes.
